      # INOUT\n197:         floating[::1] center_shift,                    # OUT\n198:         int n_threads,\n199:         bint update_centers=True):\n200:     \"\"\"Single iteration of K-means Elkan algorithm with dense input.\n201: \n202:     Update labels and centers (inplace), for one iteration, distributed\n203:     over data chunks.\n204: \n205:     Parameters\n206:     ----------\n207:     X : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_features), dtype=floating\n208:         The observations to cluster.\n209: \n210:     sample_weight : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating\n211:         The weights for each observation in X.\n212: \n213:     centers_old : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating\n214:         Centers before previous iteration, placeholder for the centers after\n215:         previous iteration.\n216: \n217:     centers_new : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_features), dtype=floating\n218:         Centers after previous iteration, placeholder for the new centers\n219:         computed during this iteration.\n220: \n221:     weight_in_clusters : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating\n222:         Placeholder for the sums of the weights of every observation assigned\n223:         to each center.\n224: \n225:     center_half_distances : ndarray of shape (n_clusters, n_clusters), \\\n226:             dtype=floating\n227:         Half pairwise distances between centers.\n228: \n229:     distance_next_center : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating\n230:         Distance between each center its closest center.\n231: \n232:     upper_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=floating\n233:         Upper bound for the distance between each sample and its center,\n234:         updated inplace.\n235: \n236:     lower_bounds : ndarray of shape (n_samples, n_clusters), dtype=floating\n237:         Lower bound for the distance between each sample and each center,\n238:         updated inplace.\n239: \n240:     labels : ndarray of shape (n_samples,), dtype=int\n241:         labels assignment.\n242: \n243:     center_shift : ndarray of shape (n_clusters,), dtype=floating\n244:         Distance between old and new centers.\n245: \n246:     n_threads : int\n247:         The number of threads to be used by openmp.\n248: \n249:     update_centers : bool\n250:         - If True, the labels and the new centers will be computed, i.e. runs\n251:           the E-step and the M-step of the algorithm.\n252:         - If False, only the labels will be computed, i.e runs the E-step of\n253:           the algorithm. This is useful especially when calling predict on a\n254:           fitted model.\n255:     \"\"\"\n256:     cdef:\n257:         int n_samples = X.shape[0]\n258:         int n_features = X.shape[1]\n259:         int n_clusters = centers_new.shape[0]\n260: \n261:     if n_samples == 0:\n262:         # An empty array was passed, do nothing and return early (before\n263:         # attempting to compute n_chunks). This can typically happen when\n264:         # calling the prediction function of a bisecting k-means model with a\n265:         # large fraction of outliers.\n266:         return\n267: \n268:     cdef:\n269:         # hard-coded number of samples per chunk. Splitting in chunks is\n270:         # necessary to get parallelism. Chunk size chosen to be same as lloyd's\n271:         int n_samples_chunk = CHUNK_SIZE if n_samples > CHUNK_SIZE else n_samples\n272:         int n_chunks = n_samples // n_samples_chunk\n273:         int n_samples_rem = n_samples % n_samples_chunk\n274:         int chunk_idx\n275:         int start, end\n276: \n277:         int i, j, k\n278: \n279:         floating *centers_new_chunk\n280:         floating *weight_in_clusters_chunk\n281: \n282:         omp_lock_t lock\n283: \n284:     # count remainder chunk in total number of chunks\n285:     n_chunks += n_samples != n_chunks * n_samples_chunk\n286: \n287:     # number of threads should not be bigger than number of chunks\n288:     n_threads = min(n_threads, n_chunks)\n289: \n290:     if update_centers:\n291:         memset(&centers_new[0, 0], 0, n_clusters * n_features * sizeof(floating))\n292:         memset(&weight_in_clusters[0], 0, n_clusters * sizeof(floating))\n293:         omp_init_lock(&lock)\n294: \n295:     with nogil, parallel(num_threads=n_threads):\n296:         # thread local buffers\n297:         centers_new_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters * n_features, sizeof(floating))\n298:         weight_in_clusters_chunk = <floating*> calloc(n_clusters, sizeof(floating))\n299: \n300:         for chunk_idx in prange(n_chunks, schedule='static'):\n301:             start = chunk_idx * n_samples_chunk\n302:             if chunk_idx == n_chunks - 1 and n_samples_rem > 0:\n303:                 end = start + n_samples_rem\n304:             else:\n305:                 end = start + n_samples_chunk\n306: \n307:             _update_chunk_dense(\n308:                 X[start: end],\n309:                 sample_weight[start: end],\n310:                 centers_old,\n311:                 center_half_distances,\n312:                 distance_next_center,\n313:                 labels[start: end],\n314:                 upper_bounds[start: end],\n315:                 lower_bounds[start: end],\n316:                 centers_new_chunk,\n317:                 weight_in_clusters_chunk,\n318:                 update_centers)\n319: \n320:         # reduction from local buffers.\n321:         if update_centers:\n322:             # The lock is necessary to avoid race conditions when aggregating\n323:             # info from different thread-local buffers.\n324:             omp_set_lock(&lock)\n325:             for j in range(n_clusters):\n326:                 weight_in_clusters[j] += weight_in_clusters_chunk[j]\n327:                 for k in range(n_features):\n328:                     centers_new[j, k] += centers_new_chunk[j * n_features + k]\n329:             omp_unset_lock(&lock)\n330: \n331:         free(centers_new_chunk)\n332:         free(weight_in_clusters_chunk)\n333: \n334:     if update_centers:\n335:         omp_destroy_lock(&lock)\n336:         _relocate_empty_clusters_dense(X, sample_weight, centers_old,\n337:                                        centers_new, weight_in_clusters, labels)\n338: \n339:         _average_centers(centers_new, weight_in_clusters)\n340:         _center_shift(centers_old, centers_new, center_shift)\n341: \n342:         # update lower and upper bounds\n343:         for i in range(n_samples):\n344:             upper_bounds[i] += center_shift[labels[i]]\n345: \n346:             for j in range(n_clusters):\n347:                 lower_bounds[i, j] -= center_shift[j]\n348:                 if lower_bounds[i, j] < 0:\n349:                     lower_bounds[i, j] = 0\n350: \n351: \n352: cdef void _update_chunk_dense(\n353:         const floating[:, ::1] X,                      # IN\n354:         const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN\n355:         const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN\n356:         const floating[:, ::1] center_half_distances,  # IN\n357:         const floating[::1] distance_next_center,      # IN\n358:         int[::1] labels,                               # INOUT\n359:         floating[::1] upper_bounds,                    # INOUT\n360:         floating[:, ::1] lower_bounds,                 # INOUT\n361:         floating *centers_new,                         # OUT\n362:         floating *weight_in_clusters,                  # OUT\n363:         bint update_centers) noexcept nogil:\n364:     \"\"\"K-means combined EM step for one dense data chunk.\n365: \n366:     Compute the partial contribution of a single data chunk to the labels and\n367:     centers.\n368:     \"\"\"\n369:     cdef:\n370:         int n_samples = labels.shape[0]\n371:         int n_clusters = centers_old.shape[0]\n372:         int n_features = centers_old.shape[1]\n373: \n374:         floating upper_bound, distance\n375:         int i, j, k, label\n376: \n377:     for i in range(n_samples):\n378:         upper_bound = upper_bounds[i]\n379:         bounds_tight = 0\n380:         label = labels[i]\n381: \n382:         # Next center is not far away from the currently assigned center.\n383:         # Sample might need to be assigned to another center.\n384:         if not distance_next_center[label] >= upper_bound:\n385: \n386:             for j in range(n_clusters):\n387: \n388:                 # If this holds, then center_index is a good candidate for the\n389:                 # sample to be relabelled, and we need to confirm this by\n390:                 # recomputing the upper and lower bounds.\n391:                 if (\n392:                     j != label\n393:                     and (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j])\n394:                     and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])\n395:                 ):\n396: \n397:                     # Recompute upper bound by calculating the actual distance\n398:                     # between the sample and its current assigned center.\n399:                     if not bounds_tight:\n400:                         upper_bound = _euclidean_dense_dense(\n401:                             &X[i, 0], &centers_old[label, 0], n_features, False)\n402:                         lower_bounds[i, label] = upper_bound\n403:                         bounds_tight = 1\n404: \n405:                     # If the condition still holds, then compute the actual\n406:                     # distance between the sample and center. If this is less\n407:                     # than the previous distance, reassign label.\n408:                     if (\n409:                         upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]\n410:                         or (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])\n411:                     ):\n412: \n413:                         distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(\n414:                             &X[i, 0], &centers_old[j, 0], n_features, False)\n415:                         lower_bounds[i, j] = distance\n416:                         if distance < upper_bound:\n417:                             label = j\n418:                             upper_bound = distance\n419: \n420:             labels[i] = label\n421:             upper_bounds[i] = upper_bound\n422: \n423:         if update_centers:\n424:             weight_in_clusters[label] += sample_weight[i]\n425:             for k in range(n_features):\n426:                 centers_new[label * n_features + k] += X[i, k] * sample_weight[i]\n427: \n428: \n429: def elkan_iter_chunked_sparse(\n430:         X,                                             # IN\n431:         const floating[::1] sample_weight,             # IN\n432:         const floating[:, ::1] centers_old,            # IN\n433:         floating[:, ::1] centers_new,                  # OUT\n434:         floating[::1] weight_in_clusters,              # If True, the labels and the new centers will be computed, i.e. runs\n494:           the E-step and the M-step of the algorithm.\n495:         - If False, only the labels will be computed, i.e runs the E-step of\n496:           the algorithm. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01UgGefQsP3qvjTc7Xmr8Hnx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Good, I can see the structure of the distance computation functions. Now let me analyze the optimization opportunities in the Elkan implementation I read.\n\nLooking at the code, I found a critical optimization opportunity:\n\nIn `_update_chunk_dense` (lines 408-411) and `_update_chunk_sparse` (lines 667-670), there's a condition using OR that should be AND:\n\n```python\nif (\n    upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]\n    or (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])\n):\n```\n\nThis should be:\n```python\nif (\n    upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]\n    and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])\n):\n```\n\nHere's why:\n\n1. **The outer condition** (lines 391-395 and 648-651) already checks that BOTH conditions are true before entering the block.\n\n2. **After tightening the bound** (when `bounds_tight == 1`), we need to re-verify both conditions.\n\n3. **Using OR is overly conservative**: It computes distance if EITHER condition is true. This can compute distances unnecessarily:\n   - If `upper_bound <= lower_bounds[i, j]`: distance(i,label) <= lower_bounds[i,j] <= distance(i,j), so j is not closer\n   - If `upper_bound <= center_half_distances[label, j]`: By triangle inequality, distance(i,j) >= distance(label,j) - distance(i,label) >= distance(label,j)/2 >= upper_bound, so j is not closer\n\n4. **Using AND is correct**: We only compute distance if BOTH conditions are true, meaning:\n   - We haven't confirmed j is worse than label\n   - AND triangle inequality still allows j to be better\n\nThis change should significantly reduce the number of distance computations (the main bottleneck in K-means).\n\n\nNow I'll prepare the patch to implement this fix.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":700,"cache_read_input_tokens":58008,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":700},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e3845722-898b-4311-b06d-4120f74ba582","uuid":"ba71979d-43ce-4b77-a013-5a5ea48290ec"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_015o3XEwAfQYg8ymboqRdcbo","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Great! Now let me verify this patch is correct by carefully analyzing the logic. Let me trace through the code path one more time to make sure I haven't made an error.\n\nLooking at lines 391-420 in _update_chunk_dense:\n\n```python\nif (    j != label\n    and (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j])\n    and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])\n):\n    # Recompute upper bound if not done yet\n    if not bounds_tight:\n        upper_bound = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)        lower_bounds[i, label] = upper_bound\n        bounds_tight = 1\n    \n    # NOW WITH MY CHANGE: use AND instead of OR    if (\n        upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]\n        and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])  # CHANGED from OR to AND\n    ):\n        distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)\n```The logic is:\n\n1. **Outer check** (line 391-395): Only enter if BOTH conditions are true:   - `upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]`: We might have a better center\n   - `upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j]`: By triangle inequality, we might have a better center\n\n2. **Inside the block**: \n   - If not tightened yet, compute exact distance to current label (lines 399-403)\n   - After potentially tightening, check conditions again\n\n3. **Inner check** (line 408, MY CHANGE): With AND, we compute distance to j ONLY if BOTH conditions are still true:\n   - `upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j]`: True means distance(i,j) > upper_bound is not guaranteed yet\n   - `upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j]`: True means triangle inequality still allows j to be better\n   \nBoth conditions must hold to warrant computing the actual distance. If either fails, we can safely skip j: when `upper_bound ≤ lower_bounds[i, j]`, we know j can't be closer since the lower bound already exceeds our best distance; when `upper_bound ≤ center_half_distances[label, j]`, the triangle inequality guarantees j is farther. Using OR would incorrectly compute distances in cases where one condition alone proves j is suboptimal. patch should change line 410 and line 669 from `or` to `and`. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01DwoCLm4kXUDPQmmSS8XEat","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01U7zAwiEGK1SL1FpFp8twwH","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/OPTIMIZATION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# K-Means Elkan Algorithm Optimization\n\n## Overview\nOptimized `sklearn.cluster._k_means._kmeans_single_elkan` by improving triangle inequality-based distance computation pruning.\n\n## Optimization Details\n\n### Problem Identified\nThe distance computation pruning logic in `_update_chunk_dense` (line 410) and `_update_chunk_sparse` (line 669) uses an **OR condition** that is overly permissive:\n\n```cython\nif (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j] or (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])):\n    distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)  # Compute distance\n```\n\nThis causes unnecessary distance computations even when both pruning conditions are satisfied.\n\n### Solution Implemented\nChanged the OR operator to AND in both functions:\n\n```cython\nif (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j] and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])):\n    distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)  # Compute distance only when necessary\n```\n\n### Why This Improves Performance\n\n**Elkan's Algorithm Pruning Rules:**\n1. If `upper_bound ≤ lower_bounds[i, j]`:\n   - Implies `distance(i, label) ≤ lower_bounds[i, j] ≤ distance(i, j)`\n   - Therefore, cluster j cannot be closer than the current assignment\n   - Safe to skip distance computation\n\n2. If `upper_bound ≤ center_half_distances[label, j]`:\n   - By triangle inequality: `distance(i, j) ≥ d(label, j) - distance(i, label) ≥ d(label, j)/2 ≥ upper_bound`\n   - Therefore, cluster j cannot be closer than the current assignment\n   - Safe to skip distance computation\n\n**Impact of OR vs AND:**\n- **OR (original)**: Computes distance if **either** condition is true → wastes computation when both are false\n- **AND (optimized)**: Computes distance only if **both** conditions are true → skips computation when either is false\n\n### Correctness\nThe optimization maintains correctness because:\n- Skipping distance computation when `upper_bound ≤ lower_bounds[i, j]` is correct (cluster j provably not closer)\n- Skipping distance computation when `upper_bound ≤ center_half_distances[label, j]` is correct (triangle inequality rules it out)\n- Both conditions must be false to skip, which is the AND condition\n\n### Complexity Reduction\n- **Before**: O(nkd) - potentially computes distance to all clusters\n- **After**: O(nkd/b) - where b is the pruning factor determined by center distances\n- The AND operator increases effective pruning power, reducing the constant factor b\n\n## Files Modified\n- `sklearn/cluster/_k_means_elkan.pyx` (2 locations)\n  - Line 410: Dense implementation\n  - Line 669: Sparse implementation\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1648,"cache_read_input_tokens":64450,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1648},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e3845722-898b-4311-b06d-4120f74ba582","uuid":"90c9c952-c8a2-481e-b224-7e79f4644e97"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01U7zAwiEGK1SL1FpFp8twwH","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /workspace/OPTIMIZATION_SUMMARY.md"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e3845722-898b-4311-b06d-4120f74ba582","uuid":"3a1f9cd9-56aa-441e-932f-6d1af24454ea","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/workspace/OPTIMIZATION_SUMMARY.md","content":"# K-Means Elkan Algorithm Optimization\n\n## Overview\nOptimized `sklearn.cluster._k_means._kmeans_single_elkan` by improving triangle inequality-based distance computation pruning.\n\n## Optimization Details\n\n### Problem Identified\nThe distance computation pruning logic in `_update_chunk_dense` (line 410) and `_update_chunk_sparse` (line 669) uses an **OR condition** that is overly permissive:\n\n```cython\nif (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j] or (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])):\n    distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)  # Compute distance\n```\n\nThis causes unnecessary distance computations even when both pruning conditions are satisfied.\n\n### Solution Implemented\nChanged the OR operator to AND in both functions:\n\n```cython\nif (upper_bound > lower_bounds[i, j] and (upper_bound > center_half_distances[label, j])):\n    distance = _euclidean_dense_dense(...)  # Compute distance only when necessary\n```\n\n### Why This Improves Performance\n\n**Elkan's Algorithm Pruning Rules:**\n1. If `upper_bound ≤ lower_bounds[i, j]`:\n   - Implies `distance(i, label) ≤ lower_bounds[i, j] ≤ distance(i, j)`\n   - Therefore, cluster j cannot be closer than the current assignment\n   - Safe to skip distance computation\n\n2. If `upper_bound ≤ center_half_distances[label, j]`:\n   - By triangle inequality: `distance(i, j) ≥ d(label, j) - distance(i, label) ≥ d(label, j)/2 ≥ upper_bound`\n   - Therefore, cluster j cannot be closer than the current assignment\n   - Safe to skip distance computation\n\n**Impact of OR vs AND:**\n- **OR (original)**: Computes distance if **either** condition is true → wastes computation when both are false\n- **AND (optimized)**: Computes distance only if **both** conditions are true → skips computation when either is false\n\n### Correctness\nThe optimization maintains correctness because:\n- Skipping distance computation when `upper_bound ≤ lower_bounds[i, j]` is correct (cluster j provably not closer)\n- Skipping distance computation when `upper_bound ≤ center_half_distances[label, j]` is correct (triangle inequality rules it out)\n- Both conditions must be false to skip, which is the AND condition\n\n### Complexity Reduction\n- **Before**: O(nkd) - potentially computes distance to all clusters\n- **After**: O(nkd/b) - where b is the pruning factor determined by center distances\n- The AND operator increases effective pruning power, reducing the constant factor b\n\n## Files Modified\n- `sklearn/cluster/_k_means_elkan.pyx` (2 locations)\n  - Line 410: Dense implementation\n  - Line 669: Sparse implementation\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
